meta_gift_desync.note: Correct-position index independently re-derived from `=`/`~` markers in quiz.gift for all 11 MCQs; sequence matches quiz.meta.json's correct_position_distribution exactly.

positional_bias.judgment: 11 items over 4 positions cannot divide evenly (11/4=2.75); {3,2,3,3} is the closest-to-uniform integer split achievable (three positions at 3, one at 2) and is not evidence of bias. Not flagged.

length_bias.note_on_builder_claim: Builder's '3/11 correct-is-longest' figure is a different, non-governing metric (raw 'is correct literally the longest option') and was independently confirmed to be accurate (q2, q4, q6) via hand-count, but the skill's actual pass/fail rule is the 1.20x-mean-of-distractors ratio, under which 0/11 questions are flagged. Both readings pass comfortably.

distractor_plausibility.note: All distractors tie to real, specific misconceptions (module-confusion e.g. math.randint/random.pi, missing parens, wrong workflow ordering, wrong file/function naming) rather than nonsense options.

verdict: Pass -- all four bias/coverage checks clean; positional and length distributions independently re-verified from raw quiz.gift markers and match the builder's sidecar claims.
